description_long: This is a large, modern cargo ship with a streamlined, angular hull painted in dark gray and black tones. The hull features a series of evenly spaced circular portholes along its length, suggesting multiple internal decks. The superstructure is positioned toward the bow and is composed of a raised, boxy bridge with a single cylindrical funnel on top. The main deck is flat and expansive, likely designed for carrying containers or bulk cargo, with minimal visible deck equipment. The bow is sharply pointed, while the stern is squared off, giving the vessel a utilitarian appearance. No visible lifeboats, cranes, or other auxiliary equipment are present on the deck.
description: A large, angular cargo ship with a dark hull, portholes, and a raised bridge with a single funnel. The deck is flat and unadorned, emphasizing its cargo-carrying function.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed bow, raised bridge, and single funnel centered on the superstructure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, flat deck, series of portholes, and the superstructure positioned toward the bow.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the squared-off stern and the full length of the cargo deck.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the portholes and superstructure.
attribute_hull_design: The hull is a single displacement type with a sharply pointed bow and a squared-off stern. The sides are flat with a slight flare, and there are no visible keels, bulbous bows, or catamaran features. The hull is punctuated by a row of circular portholes along its length.
attribute_superstructure_layout: The superstructure is a single, raised, boxy section located toward the bow, featuring a bridge and a single funnel. There are no visible additional decks, cabins, or towers beyond this main structure.
